metadata.subject: travel and tourism
metadata.content: room – a space for sleeping, e.g., We booked a double room for two nights.; double room – a hotel room for two people, e.g., They stayed in a double room near the city centre.; single room – a hotel room for one person, e.g., He prefers a single room when travelling alone.; twin‑bedded – a room with two separate beds, e.g., The twin‑bedded room is ideal for friends.; hostel – inexpensive accommodation for travelers, e.g., We stayed at a youth hostel during our backpacking trip.; guesthouse – a small private lodging, e.g., The guesthouse offered breakfast included.; resort – a place with facilities for recreation, e.g., The seaside resort attracted many tourists.; attraction – a place or event that draws visitors, e.g., The museum is a major attraction.; landmark – a well‑known building or site, e.g., The Eiffel Tower is a famous landmark.; monument – a structure erected to commemorate, e.g., The war monument stands in the central park.; ruins – remains of ancient buildings, e.g., The city tour included the ruins of the old castle.; cathedral – a large principal church, e.g., The cathedral dominates the skyline.; fountain – a decorative water feature, e.g., Children love playing near the fountain.; bridge – a structure spanning a gap, e.g., We crossed the wooden bridge over the river.; sight – a place worth seeing, e.g., The view from the hill is a breathtaking sight.; sightseeing – the activity of visiting interesting places, e.g., We spent the day sightseeing around the historic district.; traffic – the movement of vehicles, e.g., Heavy traffic caused a delay.; accident – an unexpected event causing damage, e.g., The car accident blocked the lane.; collision – a crash between vehicles, e.g., A multi‑vehicle collision occurred on the motorway.; breakdown – a mechanical failure, e.g., The bus had a breakdown on the highway.; emergency – a sudden urgent situation, e.g., Emergency services arrived quickly.; rescue – to save from danger, e.g., The rescue team helped the stranded hikers.; budget – low‑cost, e.g., We found a budget accommodation near the beach.; backpacking – traveling with a backpack, e.g., Backpacking across Europe is popular among young people.; trek – a long walk, especially in nature, e.g., They went on a mountain trek.; hike – a walk in the countryside, e.g., We plan to hike the trail tomorrow.; camp – to stay outdoors, e.g., They camped under the stars.; campsite – a place for setting up a tent, e.g., The campsite had basic facilities.; tent – portable shelter, e.g., He pitched a tent near the lake.; reservation – booking a place, e.g., We made a reservation at the hotel.; check‑in – to register upon arrival, e.g., Please check‑in at the reception.; check‑out – to leave and settle bills, e.g., We will check‑out tomorrow morning.; fully booked – no vacancies left, e.g., The hotel is fully booked for the weekend.; vacancy – an empty place, e.g., There is a vacancy in the shared dormitory.
